# The Best Chocolate Chip Cookies You'll Ever Make

I've been making these cookies for years and let me tell you, they are AMAZING. My kids ask for them every weekend. After a lot of trial and error, I've finally perfected the recipe. The secret? Brown butter and a long rest in the fridge.

## Ingredients

- 225g butter
- 200g brown sugar
- 100g caster sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 300g plain flour
- 1 tsp bicarbonate of soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 200g dark chocolate, roughly chopped

## Method

1. Brown the butter in a saucepan over medium heat until it smells nutty and turns golden. Set aside to cool slightly.

2. Whisk the sugars into the brown butter until combined.

3. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each. Add the vanilla.

4. In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, bicarb and salt.

5. Fold the dry ingredients into the wet until just combined.

6. Fold in the chopped chocolate.

7. Cover and refrigerate for at least 24 hours (up to 72 hours).

8. Scoop onto lined baking sheets and bake at 180°C for 10-12 minutes.

## Notes

Don't overbake! They should look slightly underdone when you pull them out. They'll firm up as they cool. Also, a sprinkle of flaky salt on top before baking makes a huge difference.
